docs.title: MCP Server
docs.body: The MCP server is the only write path into Canopy. Coding agents connect over the Model Context Protocol and post session output through a typed contract. Every request carries a bearer token, compared in constant time. Token rotation is tracked in #142.

versions.body: The MCP server is the only write path into Canopy. Coding agents connect over the Model Context Protocol and post session output through a typed contract. Every request carries a bearer token, compared in constant time. Token rotation is tracked in #142.
The MCP server is the only write path. Tokens are compared in constant time. Rotation: revoke and re-mint from Settings. A fresh McpServer is constructed per request.

docs.title: The reconciling gate
docs.body: Every ingested entry funnels through consume(): a replay ledger drops re-POSTs, content-hash dedupe drops identical bodies, and change-typing (new/edit/rewrite) is computed by an LCS diff against the promoted body. Low-confidence new slugs go to triage; low-confidence edits stage-and-flag.

versions.body: Every ingested entry funnels through consume(): a replay ledger drops re-POSTs, content-hash dedupe drops identical bodies, and change-typing (new/edit/rewrite) is computed by an LCS diff against the promoted body. Low-confidence new slugs go to triage; low-confidence edits stage-and-flag.

docs.title: Three auth classes
docs.body: Session cookie (humans, signed), bearer token (agents at /mcp, hashed with a canopy_mcp_ prefix), and the GitHub webhook (HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body). Each is kept strictly separate; /mcp is bearer-only and returns a bare 401 with no OAuth discovery.

versions.body: Session cookie, bearer token, webhook HMAC.

Session cookie (humans, signed), bearer token (agents at /mcp, hashed with a canopy_mcp_ prefix), and the GitHub webhook (HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body). Each is kept strictly separate; /mcp is bearer-only and returns a bare 401 with no OAuth discovery.

docs.title: My Work projection
docs.body: My Work is a D1-only projection over captured events: previousActivity (summarized merged/closed PRs) and todo (open assigned issues, 5 most recent). Each PR and issue is summarized once at capture time into structured columns (PR: title/what/why/impact; issue: title/summary/next_step).

docs.title: Product Overview
docs.body: Canopy is the shared source of truth and working memory for Sapling, a four-person software team. Agents propose context through a reconciling gate; humans confirm the consequential changes in Triage.

versions.body: Canopy is the shared source of truth and working memory for Sapling, a four-person software team. Agents propose context through a reconciling gate; humans confirm the consequential changes in Triage.

docs.title: How the team works
docs.body: Orient with load-context before touching an area, work, then record-session at the end to stage one reconciled batch. Trust live results; scrutinize anything staged, unpromoted, or draft.

versions.body: Orient with load-context before touching an area, work, then record-session at the end to stage one reconciled batch. Trust live results; scrutinize anything staged, unpromoted, or draft.

docs.title: Onboarding a new agent
docs.body: Get added to the SaplingLearn org, sign in to Canopy, then mint an MCP bearer token in Settings. Point your agent at /mcp with the bearer; the connection is stateless.

versions.body: Get added to the SaplingLearn org, sign in to Canopy, then mint an MCP bearer token in Settings. Point your agent at /mcp with the bearer; the connection is stateless.

docs.title: ADR-001 · D1 for the store
docs.body: Use a single Cloudflare D1 database as the store. Sections, versions, feed entries, events, and decisions are all rows. One Worker on one origin serves the API, MCP, webhook, and static web.

versions.body: Use a single Postgres instance as the store.
Use a single Cloudflare D1 database as the store. Sections, versions, feed entries, events, and decisions are all rows. One Worker on one origin serves the API, MCP, webhook, and static web.
versions.summary: Switch Postgres to D1 after the Workers move

docs.title: Sapling platform architecture
docs.body: The Sapling learning platform is a Next.js frontend backed by a Workers API and D1. Lessons are authored as MDX, compiled at build, and served from the edge. Progress and streaks live in Durable Objects.

versions.body: The Sapling learning platform is a Next.js frontend backed by a Workers API and D1. Lessons are authored as MDX, compiled at build, and served from the edge. Progress and streaks live in Durable Objects.

docs.title: Content pipeline
docs.body: Authors write lessons in MDX in the sapling repo. A GitHub Action validates frontmatter against the schema, renders previews, and on merge publishes to R2. Broken links fail the check.

versions.body: Authors write lessons in MDX in the sapling repo. A GitHub Action validates frontmatter against the schema, renders previews, and on merge publishes to R2. Broken links fail the check.

docs.title: Sapling public API
docs.body: The public API exposes /courses, /lessons, and /progress. Auth is a per-learner JWT. Rate limits are 120 req/min per token. All responses are cache-friendly with ETags.

versions.body: The public API exposes /courses, /lessons, and /progress. Auth is a per-learner JWT. Rate limits are 120 req/min per token. All responses are cache-friendly with ETags.
The public API exposes /courses, /lessons, /progress, and /certificates. Auth is a per-learner JWT with refresh. Rate limits are 120 req/min per token; 429s carry Retry-After.
